name: Remote: Client Based DNS Activity to the Internet
notes: This rule triggers when a host is attempting to connect to a DNS server that is not defined as a local network. With the exception of your DNS servers or other hosts specifically configured to communicate with remote DNS servers, this is suspicious activity and may be the sign of a botnet connection. If this is a false positive, add the remote DNS server to the BB HostDefinition: DNS Servers building block in the custom rules.
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Remote
when a flow matches any of the following BB:Threats: DoS: Potential Multihost Attack
when the flow matches Application is any of [Misc.DNS or Misc.domain]
TD_list: APPLY Remote: Client Based DNS Activity to the Internet on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow context is Local to Remote
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:Threats: DoS: Potential Multihost Attack
AND when the flow matches Application is any of [Misc.DNS or Misc.domain]
RED_list: Event name: Client Based DNS Activity to the Internet

Event description: Detects a host attempting to connect to a DNS server that is not defined as a local network.  With the exception of your DNS servers or other hosts specifically configured to communicate with remote DNS servers, this is suspicious activity and may be the sign of a bot net connection.  If this is a false positive, add the remote DNS server to the BB HostDefinition: DNS Servers building block in the custom rules.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
